policy: Historical analogs compare configuration patterns, not isolated chart shapes. Similarity scores are provisional until a real statistical matching engine is wired to long-history data. Evidence quality must be disclosed for every analog.

analogs.label: Inflation shock / real-yield repricing
analogs.pattern: Restrictive money, inflation pressure, strong nominal activity, risk-asset valuation compression risk.
analogs.difference: Current credit stress is less severe and AI infrastructure leadership remains more concentrated.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Do not assume multiple expansion while real yields remain restrictive; size risk around inflation and rate sensitivity.

analogs.label: Funding stress without immediate broad collapse
analogs.pattern: Strong risk appetite coexisted with dollar/funding pressure until stress became more visible.
analogs.difference: Current physical constraint / AI infrastructure cycle is structurally different from late-1990s internet leadership.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Watch funding markets and dollar stress carefully; contained credit can delay but not eliminate risk repricing.

analogs.label: Tight money slowdown
analogs.pattern: Restrictive policy, equity compression, late-cycle pressure, and recession risk emerged after prolonged tightening.
analogs.difference: Modern market structure, central bank reaction function, and technology concentration differ materially.
analogs.portfolioLesson: High money restriction can matter with a lag; avoid assuming resilience means no downside.

analogs.label: Oil shock / physical constraint recession
analogs.pattern: Physical constraints and inflation pressure interacted with restrictive policy and equity drawdown risk.
analogs.difference: Current physical pressure is broader and includes power, copper, uranium, and AI infrastructure rather than only oil.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Physical constraints can convert inflation into margin pressure; energy and infrastructure exposure may matter.

analogs.label: Real-rate reset
analogs.pattern: Restrictive real rates forced repricing across duration, credit, and cyclical exposure.
analogs.difference: Inflation regime and policy credibility differ; current equity leadership is more concentrated around technology infrastructure.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Treat duration and weak balance sheets cautiously when real-rate pressure remains high.

analogs.label: Credit collapse / deflation break
analogs.pattern: Equity leadership failed as credit deterioration became dominant.
analogs.difference: Current credit axis is contained; policy regime and monetary backstop are structurally different.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Use as tail-risk memory only; not a base-case analog unless credit deterioration accelerates.

missingEvidence: Pre-1960 proxy normalization for credit spreads
Comparable real-yield proxy before TIPS era
Power / electricity demand long-history comparable series
